name: avalonia-zafiro-development
description: Mandatory skills, conventions, and behavioral rules for Avalonia UI development using the Zafiro toolkit.
capabilities: Enforce functional-reactive MVVM architecture patterns using DynamicData and ReactiveUI
Provide naming and coding standards for consistent C# development
Guide validation patterns using Zafiro extensions and Result types
Offer shortcut references for common Rx and DynamicData operations
Define cross-platform UI development rules for Avalonia
Prevent common anti-patterns in reactive collection handling
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. This skill contains only markdown documentation for Avalonia UI development conventions. The flagged patterns (backtick execution, weak crypto, system reconnaissance) are triggered by markdown syntax and development terminology in documentation context, not actual executable code. No scripts, network calls, file system access, or environment variable usage present.
valueStatement: This skill enforces mandatory conventions for Avalonia UI development with Zafiro toolkit, ensuring maintainable functional-reactive MVVM code that follows industry best practices.
limitations: Does not execute code or provide runtime functionality
Requires existing knowledge of C# and MVVM patterns
Does not include Zafiro library implementation details
Focuses on conventions rather than complete implementation guides

useCases.targetUser: Developers new to Avalonia Zafiro development
useCases.title: New Avalonia Project Setup
useCases.description: Apply Zafiro conventions when starting a new Avalonia UI project to establish proper architecture from the beginning.

useCases.title: Code Review and Refactoring
useCases.description: Use the skill to review existing code against Zafiro patterns and identify areas needing refactoring.

useCases.title: Learning Reactive Patterns
useCases.description: Reference the shortcuts and patterns when implementing DynamicData pipelines or reactive commands.

promptTemplates.title: Apply Zafiro Conventions
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply the Avalonia Zafiro development conventions to review and improve this code. Focus on MVVM purity, DynamicData usage, and Zafiro helper methods.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have C# code that needs to follow Zafiro patterns

promptTemplates.title: Fix Reactive Anti-Patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Identify and fix reactive anti-patterns in this code using Zafiro best practices. Look for ad-hoc SourceList creation, logic in Subscribe, and improper operator usage.

promptTemplates.title: Implement Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement validation using Zafiro ValidationRule and extensions. Show the proper pattern for validating dynamic collections.
promptTemplates.scenario: When adding form or data validation

promptTemplates.title: Optimize Collection Handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply RefreshableCollection pattern and DynamicData operators to optimize collection handling in this code.
promptTemplates.scenario: When working with dynamic lists and collections

bestPractices: Prefer DynamicData operators over plain Rx operators when working with collections
Use Result types from CSharpFunctionalExtensions for error handling instead of exceptions
Keep ViewModels strictly Avalonia-independent for cross-platform compatibility
antiPatterns: Creating SourceList or SourceCache on the fly for local problems instead of using existing helpers
Placing business logic inside Subscribe blocks instead of in the reactive pipeline
Using plain System.Reactive operators when DynamicData equivalents exist

faq.question: What is Zafiro in Avalonia development?
faq.answer: Zafiro is a toolkit that provides abstractions, helpers, and extension methods for Avalonia UI development, focusing on reactive patterns and clean architecture.

faq.question: Why use DynamicData over plain Rx?
faq.answer: DynamicData provides specialized operators for collection management (Connect, Filter, Transform, Sort) that are more efficient and idiomatic for UI binding scenarios.

faq.question: What does pure MVVM mean in this context?
faq.answer: ViewModels must not reference Avalonia types directly, ensuring they remain testable and platform-independent.

faq.question: When should I use Result types?
faq.answer: Use Result types for expected error paths and flow control. Reserve exceptions for truly exceptional, unrecoverable situations.

faq.question: What is RefreshableCollection?
faq.answer: A Zafiro pattern that manages refreshable lists with automatic loading, efficient updates via EditDiff, and ReadOnlyObservableCollection output for UI binding.

faq.question: How do I handle errors in reactive pipelines?
faq.answer: Use Zafiro's HandleErrorsWith extension to pipe errors directly to the UI notification service instead of manual Subscribe error handling.
